name: Austin, Texas

rent.notes: Texas has no state income tax but higher property taxes reflected in rent. Water, sewage, trash typically separate.
rent.gigInternetNotes: Highly competitive fiber market with Google Fiber + AT&T. 73-79% coverage depending on provider. Fiber common in newer developments; some older central neighborhoods cable-only.

groceries.notes: Austin grocery prices near national average. H-E-B is dominant and competitively priced. No sales tax on groceries in Texas.

electricity.notes: Deregulated market. Austin Energy municipal utility. Heavy A/C usage May-Sep ($200-280). Winter mild ($80-120).

gas.notes: Texas Gas Service. Minimal heating needs. Cooking and water heater only for most of year.

water.notes: Austin Water. Tiered pricing, 2-adult household ~5,000 gal/mo.

sewage.notes: Billed with water. Austin Water wastewater charges.

trash.notes: Austin Resource Recovery. Curbside pickup included in utility bill ~$25/mo.

utilities.taxes: Texas has no state income tax. Residential electricity subject to various rider fees but no state sales tax on utilities.
utilities.notes: Electricity (heating+cooling), gas, water, sewage, trash. Internet is in entertainment (Cable+1Gig).

healthcare.notes: Medicare Part B ($174.70/mo) + Medigap Plan G (~$200/mo x2 people) + Part D (~$45/mo x2) + dental/vision plans. Higher premiums reflect macular degeneration specialist coverage and frequent monitoring needs.

petCare.notes: Austin is extremely dog-friendly. Numerous vet clinics. Emancipet provides low-cost vet care.

petDaycare.notes: Doggy daycare 2 days/week, large breed rate (~$44/day). Camp Bow Wow, Taurus Academy, Barking Springs.

petGrooming.notes: Bernese coat maintenance, professional grooming monthly.

transportation.notes: Car-dependent. CapMetro bus/rail expanding. Gas prices moderate. No state vehicle inspection fee for new cars. Insurance rates moderate.

entertainment.notes: Incl cable+1Gig internet, Netflix, Amazon Prime, Apple TV+, dining out (lunch for 2 2x/week w/ tax+tip). Live music capital — many free shows.

phoneCell.notes: T-Mobile 55+ Essentials. 2 phones. TX wireless tax ~8%.

miscellaneous.notes: Tolls (183A, MoPac Express), HOA common in suburbs, parking generally free.

buffer.notes: Monthly safety buffer for unexpected expenses

medicalOOP.notes: Anti-VEGF injections for macular degeneration (~$300-400 OOP each, 6-10x/yr after Medicare 80%). Specialist visits: ophthalmologist q6-8wks, endocrinologist 2-4x/yr, cardiologist 1-2x/yr. Frequent labs: lipid panel, TSH, A1C, CMP 3-4x/yr each. Both adults: PCP visits quarterly.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

taxes.notes: Estimated monthly income tax on $72,000/yr household income. Based on jurisdiction's effective retiree rate; excludes sales/VAT/property tax.

healthcarePreMedicare.notes: ACA silver benchmark for 2-adult household both age ~60, before subsidies. County-level estimate (Travis County, TX). Austin metro — Ambetter, Oscar, Blue Cross TX. Estimated ±15% vs healthcare.gov quote — verify for a specific ZIP before relying on the number.

stateIncomeTax.exemptions: Texas has no state income tax.

salesTax.notes: Austin 8.25% (6.25% state + 2% local). No sales tax on groceries.

propertyTax.notes: Renters: no direct property tax. Texas property tax is among the highest in the US (~1.8-2.2% of home value in Travis County), reflected in rent.

taxes.notes: No state income tax. Higher property tax and sales tax compensate. SS not taxed at state level (no state income tax).

healthcare.notes: Dell Seton Medical Center (Level I trauma), St. David's Medical Center, Ascension Seton network. UT Dell Medical School expanding. Good specialist access.

acaMarketplace.notes: Austin metro — Ambetter, Oscar, Blue Cross TX.
acaMarketplace.disclaimer: Estimated ±15% vs healthcare.gov quote — verify for a specific ZIP before relying on the number.

lifestyle.notes: Zilker Park, Lady Bird Lake, Barton Creek Greenbelt, Red Bud Isle (off-leash dog park). Live music capital. Tech hub culture. Keep Austin Weird. Very dog-friendly city.

pros: Live music scene — many free events
Strong tech economy and modern amenities

cons.text: Extreme summer heat (97F+ highs, 100+ heat index)
Rising cost of living due to tech migration
Heavy traffic congestion on I-35 and MoPac
High property taxes reflected in rent
Flash flood risk in hill country areas
Safety & crime: see cited reports for current figures by city and country
